The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum acutangulum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, decoctions, and infusions, as well as being used in traditional Ayurvedic formulations such as churnas and kwathas.
